title: dysphania platycarpa
intro: Dysphania platycarpa, also known as Fat-hen, is a type of annual or biennial plant that belongs to the amaranth family and is characterized by its broad, flat seeds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Dysphania platycarpa include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain infections, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Dysphania platycarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some food products, such as soups and stews, due to its nutty flavor and high nutritional value.
